title: Geopolitical tension in the Persian Gulf: US attacks Iran, oil soars and markets fall
summary: Oil exceeds $85 following US bombings and Trump's threat to impose tolls on ships in Hormuz, causing a slump in global stock markets and a rise in gold and silver as safe-haven assets.

title: US inflation expectations and Fed comments focus market attention
summary: The market awaits US CPI and statements by Kevin Warsh and Governor Waller on possible rate hikes, while European and Japanese sovereign bonds remain stable and Euribor loses steam.

title: SK Hynix plunges more than 15% in Seoul after record debut on Wall Street
summary: SK Hynix shares tumble sharply after its ADR listing in the US gave back all initial gains, as analysts debate whether the semiconductor rally has ended.

title: Meta, Nvidia and Microsoft boost investment in artificial intelligence and data centers
summary: Meta explores a cloud business, Nvidia partners with Mitsubishi Heavy for AI data centers, Microsoft promises parabolic growth, and TSMC reports a 68% revenue increase, reinforcing investor confidence in the sector.

title: Financial regulatory bodies in several countries announce measures and reforms
summary: Spain's CNMV reduces regulatory burdens, Germany's BaFin quantifies the Cum-Cum scandal costs at 7 billion euros, and the English and Welsh legal regulator is harshly criticized for losing direction.

title: Large corporations face million-dollar lawsuits in the United States and Europe
summary: California leads a lawsuit to block the Warner Bros/Paramount merger, a court revives lawsuits against Kenvue over Tylenol, and three German banks sue Linde for 800 million euros over a project in Russia.

title: Evotec plummets 32% after widening losses and cutting annual forecasts
summary: The German biotech company revises down its outlook for 2026, causing a sharp drop in its stock price.

title: Bitcoin suffers a 2.11% drop and retreats after a bullish opening
summary: The leading cryptocurrency depreciates amid rising risk aversion due to geopolitical tensions and macroeconomic uncertainty.

title: Lifco increases its net profit in the second quarter and its shares rise
summary: The Swedish industrial services company reports profit growth, pushing its stock price higher.

title: Chinese exports surge in June and Asian stock markets close mixed
summary: China's export data beats expectations, while Asian markets react with mixed results to global trade prospects.

title: Volkswagen suggests additional 50,000 job cuts in its restructuring plan
summary: The German carmaker continues its restructuring and proposes new mass layoffs to improve competitiveness, according to a letter to employees.

title: India pressures banks to attract diaspora deposits amid rupee weakness
summary: The Indian government urges financial institutions to attract funds from non-residents to counteract the depreciation of the local currency.

title: US small-cap stocks rebound as interest rotates from big tech
summary: The Russell 2000 index rises in a sign of sector rotation, as investors seek opportunities in smaller companies.

title: Wall Street's 'Super Tuesday' marks the start of earnings season
summary: Several large companies report their quarterly results, offering an initial look at corporate health in the second quarter of 2026.

title: UK reaches agreement with EU to participate in joint loan for aid to Ukraine
summary: The agreement allows the UK to join the European mechanism for financial support to Ukraine, strengthening international backing amid the war.

title: Shell sells its wind and solar business in India for $1.8 billion
summary: The Anglo-Dutch oil company divests its renewable energy assets in India as part of its strategy to focus on more profitable projects.

title: Telefónica rises 2.5% on the Ibex driven by the 'Vodafone effect'
summary: Shares of the Spanish telecom operator benefit from expectations generated by corporate moves in the telecommunications sector.

title: MUFG becomes the most valuable company in Japan for the first time in 40 years
summary: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group surpasses Toyota and other major companies in market capitalization, driven by rising interest rates and financial stability.

title: Moneybox chooses London's Pisces market to place employee shares
summary: The British fintech opts for the London Stock Exchange's alternative market for the sale of employee equity stakes.

title: Nikkei surges more than 500 points as investor confidence returns in Asia
summary: The Japanese stock market closes with strong gains, driven by a rebound in tech stocks and improved sentiment in South Korea, while the JPX 400 and Growth 250 indices also rise.
